item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1, but I do think this article shows some good writing. I just think the idea is holding it back.

This had been said plenty of times, but strange tumors that cover the patient is a bit played out, and is done better. Also, I get the intention behind the letters at the end of the article, but I also think the pun is a bit too much on the nose.

What I will say is that I like how the -1 in this article isn't just in a total panic he's actually trying to provide some information to the Foundation. That's something that doesn't often happen with these types of articles.

That being said, with anything more serious than what's going on here, I don't think that would work.

All in all, there's some good execution but a rather weak idea. TBQH, the idea is the hardest part of SCP articles, so don't get too discouraged!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'Weird tumours' is kind of played out by this point, and while 'someone turned this guy into a disease' is a nice hook, I'm tired of seeing articles where the story boils down to 'somebody gets obliterated by an anomaly for super-petty reasons because the universe/a PoI decided to mess them up'. Also seconding Petrograd's comment that the interview doesn't add all that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your downvote is respected; thank you for letting me know your thoughts on the article.

I do believe that the interview serves to paint a slightly better picture overall, although it could have been pulled off better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, the physical description definitely grossed me out, but it's not super original.

I really don't see what the interview adds. Pretty much just, "This is unpleasant for the -1s," and that's it. No new narrative. The texts aren't particularly realistic, and mostly just show that it's a pun.

There's really not much of a story going on here. Gross horrible disease originates from an unpleasant person. That's about it. Vivid mental imagery is worth something, but not enough here.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feel like I've read the tumor turning into people thing here before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suitably bonkers (although there's a touch of Convenient Expositionary Note).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This idea just hit me one day, in which the universe decided to act up.

Hope you like the article.

Big thanks to Pendleton_Miller for the help in reviewing it.
